sanskrit: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ। तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ॥
transliteration: indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣau vyavasthitau | tayor na vaśam āgacchet tau hy asya paripanthinau ||
literal_meaning: For every sense object there is an established pair—attraction and aversion; do not come under their control, for they are the obstructers on the path.
contextual_meaning: The nervous system is wired to tag experiences as ‘like’ or ‘dislike’. These reflexes are not moral flaws; they become problems only when we obey them uncritically. Self-realization demands that we notice the impulse before it becomes a decision.
psychological_state: High vigilance—Arjuna is aware that his own nervous system can hijack his values, so he feels the tension between biological impulse and chosen purpose.
trigger_scenario: You open Instagram for ‘five minutes’ and 45 minutes vanish; the same thumb that clicked ‘like’ now feels vaguely dirty. The verse is the pause button between stimulus and swipe.
anchor_text: Notice the pull of like and dislike, then choose; don’t let them choose for you.
